name: Master of Education in Curriculum & Instruction
description: Prepare to design and evaluate curriculum for K-12 and adult learning environments. Focuses on instructional design, assessment strategies, and educational technology integration.
outcomes: Design evidence-based curricula aligned with state and national standards
Evaluate instructional programs using data-driven methodologies
Integrate educational technology into teaching practice
Lead curriculum reform initiatives at the school or district level
requirements: Bachelor's degree from an accredited institution
Minimum 2.5 cumulative GPA
Teaching license or relevant professional experience
Statement of purpose

name: Master of Education in Educational Leadership
description: Develop the skills to lead schools, districts, and educational organizations. Covers leadership theory, school finance, policy analysis, and community engagement.
outcomes: Apply leadership theories to real-world educational settings
Manage school budgets and resource allocation effectively
Navigate educational policy at local, state, and federal levels
Build inclusive school cultures that support all learners
requirements: Bachelor's degree from an accredited institution
Minimum 3.0 cumulative GPA
Two years of teaching or educational experience
Two professional recommendation letters

name: Master of Arts in Teaching
description: Designed for career changers and aspiring teachers. Combines pedagogical theory with extensive field experience to prepare you for initial licensure.
outcomes: Demonstrate proficiency in content-area teaching methods
Create inclusive and culturally responsive classrooms
Apply formative and summative assessment strategies
Meet state requirements for initial teaching licensure
requirements: Bachelor's degree from an accredited institution
Minimum 2.75 cumulative GPA
Background check clearance
Content area proficiency (Praxis or equivalent)

name: Bachelor of Science in Business Administration
description: Build a strong foundation in management, marketing, finance, and operations. Prepares graduates for leadership roles across industries.
outcomes: Analyze business problems using quantitative and qualitative methods
Develop strategic plans for organizational growth
Apply ethical decision-making frameworks in business contexts
Communicate effectively in professional settings
requirements: High school diploma or equivalent
No minimum GPA required for admission
Official transcripts from all prior institutions

name: Bachelor of Science in Healthcare Administration
description: Prepare for management roles in hospitals, clinics, insurance companies, and public health organizations. Covers health policy, informatics, and operations management.
outcomes: Manage healthcare delivery systems and operations
Navigate healthcare regulations and compliance requirements
Apply health informatics tools for decision-making
Lead quality improvement initiatives in healthcare settings
requirements: High school diploma or equivalent
Official transcripts from all prior institutions
Recommended: experience in a healthcare setting

name: Associate of Science in Information Technology
description: An accelerated pathway into IT careers. Covers networking fundamentals, cybersecurity basics, database management, and help desk operations.
outcomes: Configure and troubleshoot computer networks
Implement basic cybersecurity measures
Manage relational databases using SQL
Provide effective technical support in enterprise environments
requirements: High school diploma or equivalent
No minimum GPA required

name: Certificate in Data Analytics
description: A focused credential in data analysis, visualization, and statistical reasoning. Ideal for professionals looking to add analytical skills to their career toolkit.
outcomes: Clean, transform, and analyze datasets using industry tools
Create compelling data visualizations for stakeholder communication
Apply statistical methods to business decision-making
Build dashboards and automated reports
requirements: Bachelor's degree or equivalent professional experience
Basic proficiency in spreadsheet software

name: Doctor of Education in Organizational Leadership
description: A practitioner-focused doctoral program for experienced leaders. Integrates research methodology with applied leadership challenges in education, business, and nonprofit sectors.
outcomes: Conduct original applied research addressing organizational challenges
Lead transformative change in complex organizations
Apply advanced theories of leadership, ethics, and social justice
Contribute to scholarly and practitioner literature
requirements: Master's degree from an accredited institution
Minimum 3.25 cumulative GPA in graduate work
Five years of professional leadership experience
Writing sample and interview
